prerequisites: A Snowflake account with support for vector type columns, where you have created (or can access) a database, warehouse, default schema, and role with privileges to create and write to tables.
A Snowflake username and password for the role that will load data (mapped to the indexing.credentials.password secret field).
An account with API access for an embedding provider (OpenAI or Cohere), unless you use the built-in Fake embeddings option for testing.
An OpenAI or Cohere API key for the chosen embedding method (mapped to embedding.openai_key, embedding.cohere_key, or embedding.api_key).
A source whose records expose the text fields and (optionally) metadata fields you intend to embed, so they can be referenced in the processing config.

authMethods.name: Snowflake username and password
authMethods.summary: The destination authenticates to Snowflake with a username and password. The password is supplied through the indexing.credentials.password secret field, alongside the Snowflake host (account identifier), role, warehouse, database, default schema, and username in the indexing config.

authMethods.name: OpenAI embedding API key
authMethods.summary: When the embedding method is OpenAI, the connector calls the OpenAI embeddings API (text-embedding-ada-002, 1536 dimensions) using an API key provided in the embedding.openai_key secret field. The generic embedding.api_key secret field holds the embedding service API key.

authMethods.name: Cohere embedding API key
authMethods.summary: When the embedding method is Cohere, the connector calls the Cohere embeddings API (embed-english-light-v2.0, 1024 dimensions) using an API key provided in the embedding.cohere_key secret field.

setupSteps.title: Prepare the Snowflake target objects
setupSteps.body: In Snowflake, ensure you have a database, warehouse, default schema, and role available for the load, and that the role can create and write to tables. Note the host (the account identifier before .snowflakecomputing.com), role, warehouse, database, and default schema; you will enter these in the indexing config.

setupSteps.title: Create or identify the Snowflake user credentials
setupSteps.body: Use a Snowflake username and password for a user granted the load role. The password is stored in the indexing.credentials.password secret field.

setupSteps.title: Configure the indexing section
setupSteps.body: Fill in the indexing config with the Snowflake host, role, warehouse, database, default schema, username, and password (indexing.credentials.password). This is the destination Snowflake Cortex will write embedded vectors to.

setupSteps.title: Choose an embedding method and provide its API key
setupSteps.body: In the embedding config, select OpenAI, Cohere, or Fake. For OpenAI, supply an API key in embedding.openai_key (text-embedding-ada-002, 1536 dimensions). For Cohere, supply an API key in embedding.cohere_key (embed-english-light-v2.0, 1024 dimensions). The embedding.api_key field holds the embedding service API key. Use Fake only for testing, which requires no key.

setupSteps.title: Configure processing of source records
setupSteps.body: In the processing config, specify the text fields to embed (dot notation and wildcards are supported) and any metadata fields, and set the chunk length in tokens (maximum 8191). These define how source records are split and embedded.

setupSteps.title: Optionally omit raw text
setupSteps.body: Set omit_raw_text to true if you do not want the original raw text stored alongside the vectors in Snowflake. Leave it unset or false to retain raw text.
